message:
fix(replay): extend oracle replay match to compare full lab result fields

Expand asx_oracle_replay_match() to check two additional lab result
fields that were previously ignored:
  - steps_total: ensures the total step contract (not just completed
    count) matches between replay runs
  - polls_total: ensures the number of scheduler poll cycles matches,
    catching divergences in polling behavior even when step counts agree

Update existing test fixture (oracle_replay_match_pass) to populate the
new fields, and add two new focused test cases:
  - oracle_replay_match_steps_total_diverge: verifies detection when
    steps_total differs
  - oracle_replay_match_polls_diverge: verifies detection when
    polls_total differs

Beads issue tracker updated with bd-j1be (in_progress) tracking this
replay oracle enhancement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ix8.6","title":"Implement compile-time memory-layout and budget-delta reporting per target","description":"Background:
Plan calls for per-target visibility into memory layout and budget drift to protect constrained deployments.

Subtasks:
- Generate compile-time reports for key structs and allocator budgets per target.
- Diff reports across target matrix and flag regressions.
- Integrate report artifacts into CI outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Reports are reproducible and machine-comparable.
- Budget deltas are surfaced before deployment regressions.
- Embedded classes can be tuned with concrete evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.388405193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:41:56.256821641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:41:56.256803306Z","close_reason":"Completed: compile-time layout + budget delta reporting integrated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","embedded","idea-20","memory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.7","title":"Implement endian and unaligned-access hardening suite for binary transport paths","description":"Background:
Binary codec correctness depends on explicit handling of endian and alignment differences.

Subtasks:
- Add compile/runtime checks for endian assumptions.
- Add tests and fixtures for unaligned access edge cases.
- Verify binary decode behavior on target matrix and emulated runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No undefined behavior from alignment/endian assumptions.
- Edge fixtures pass consistently across matrix targets.
- Failures produce actionable diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.494070205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:42:58.547008391Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:42:58.546987291Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","codec","idea-20","portability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-296.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.8","title":"Implement Phase 2.5 walking skeleton end-to-end lifecycle path","description":"Background:
Plan phase 2.5 adds a mandatory end-to-end smoke path to surface integration mismatches early.

Subtasks:
- Implement minimal region open/close + task create/poll-ready path through real public headers.
- Implement trivial scheduler loop that can drive one no-op task to completion and assert quiescence.
- Add deterministic integration test and run manifest/log output for portability matrix + QEMU targets.
- Keep implementation minimal and explicitly non-final, focused on proving layer wiring.

Acceptance Criteria:
- One real lifecycle path executes correctly across required CI and QEMU targets.
- Integration test artifacts are deterministic and reproducible.
- Phase-3 expansion work uses this skeleton as baseline safety net.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:24:55.241073418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:08:00.037263907Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:08:00.037244581Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","integration","phase-2","smoke"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-j1be","title":"Fix replay oracle to compare full lab results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T01:47:13.375120343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T01:48:21.453391301Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T01:48:21.452627415Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-j4m","title":"EPIC: Cross-Vertical Profiles (Embedded Router, HFT, Automotive) with Zero Semantic Drift","description":"Background:
This project must serve multiple hard domains without splitting semantics into incompatible forks.

Goal:
Implement profile-specific operational tuning while enforcing semantic digest equivalence across shared fixtures.

Includes:
- embedded resource classes (R1/R2/R3)
- HFT tail/jitter governance
- automotive deadline/watchdog/degraded-mode behavior
- vertical adapters with fallback isomorphism artifacts

Why this serves users:
- domain-specific utility without losing the core architecture
- confidence that profile tuning does not compromise correctness.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","notes":"All 8 child tasks completed:
- bd-j4m.1: Profile compatibility baseline (CLOSED)
- bd-j4m.2: Embedded router resource classes (CLOSED)
- bd-j4m.3: HFT profile instrumentation (CLOSED)
- bd-j4m.4: Automotive profile instrumentation (CLOSED)
- bd-j4m.5: Vertical acceleration adapters (CLOSED)
- bd-j4m.6: Deployment hardening playbooks (CLOSED, CoralValley)
- bd-j4m.7: OpenWrt packaging kit (CLOSED, LilacHawk)
- bd-j4m.8: Overload policy catalog (CLOSED)
This unblocks bd-66l (Hard Quality Gates EPIC).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.317289058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T03:27:39.566298569Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T03:27:39.566202699Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automotive","embedded","hft","phase-8","phase-9","profiles"]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.1","title":"Implement profile compatibility baseline and cross-profile semantic parity enforcement","description":"Background:
The project must avoid domain forks. Profiles may tune operations but not semantics.

Subtasks:
- Define shared-fixture parity set across core, freestanding, embedded_router, hft, and automotive profiles.
- Implement profile compatibility checks and canonical digest comparisons.
- Document acceptable operational differences versus forbidden semantic differences.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Profile parity runner reports pass/fail with first divergence detail.
- Compatibility rules are explicit and machine-checkable.
- Any semantic drift is blocked by default.","notes":"COMPLETED: profile compatibility baseline implemented. include/asx/runtime/profile_compat.h (public API), src/runtime/profile_compat.c (implementation), tests/unit/runtime/test_profile_compat.c (33/33 tests pass). Deliverables: 7 runtime-queryable profile identities, operational descriptors for all profiles, 8 semantic rules (all enforced), digest-based parity comparison, self-parity verification via telemetry digest, cross-profile descriptor divergence validation. Full build clean, 42/43 tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in test_stress).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.609378813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:26:04.808491649Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T22:26:01.903935599Z","close_reason":"Completed: shared-fixture parity baseline now covers ASX_PROFILE_CORE/FREESTANDING/EMBEDDED_ROUTER/HFT/AUTOMOTIVE (JSON+BIN); strict conformance/codec-equivalence/profile-parity all pass with comparable pairs and zero diffs. Added explicit compatibility contract (allowed operational differences vs forbidden semantic drift) in docs/SAFETY_PROFILES.md and updated FEATURE_PARITY status tracking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compatibility","parity","profiles"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.1","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-j4m.2","title":"Implement embedded_router profile with resource classes and wear-aware diagnostics","description":"Background:
Embedded utility requires low-memory operation and flash-safe diagnostics without losing core semantics.

Subtasks:
- Implement embedded_router defaults for constrained targets.
- Implement resource classes R1, R2, R3 with deterministic reject/backpressure behavior.
- Implement RAM-ring trace mode and wear-aware persistent spill strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Router profile passes semantic parity fixtures.
- Resource ceiling failures are deterministic and non-corrupting.
- Diagnostic mode behavior is documented for low-flash devices.","notes":"2026-02-28: Implemented resource class enum (ASX_CLASS_R1/R2/R3) in asx_config.h. Added asx_profile_get_descriptor_for_class() for resource-class-scaled descriptors. Implemented trace config (asx_trace_config) with RAM_RING/PERSISTENT_SPILL modes, wear-safe flag, and per-class ring capacity defaults. 55 tests (22 new) all passing. Descriptor/runtime limit alignment resolved via ASX_MAX_* macro normalization.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacHawk","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:40.712177293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-28T01:32:20.091125059Z","closed_at":"2026-02-28T01:23:19.676531019Z","close_reason":"Completed: descriptor/runtime alignment + active profile wait defaults; rch build/format/lint/test/conformance/profile-parity green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","profiles","router"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-j4m.2","depends_on_id":"bd-j4m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:37:11Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
